content:
# Jauf Formation

**Summary**: A Devonian sedimentary formation in Saudi Arabia.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] studied the Jauf Formation as a primary hydrocarbon reservoir.

- [[2001-al-subai|Al-Subai, 2001]] studied the Jauf formation to evaluate gas condensate distribution.

## Depositional Environment

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] shallow marine, barrier island, and associated channel sandstones

- [[2001-al-subai|Al-Subai, 2001]] studied the Jauf formation to evaluate gas condensate distribution.

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] compaction, cementation (calcite, quartz, illite, chlorite), and dissolution

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The formation is characterized by 77% quartz, 2% feldspar, and 0.1% lithic fragments. ([[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001, p. 31]])

- The reservoir is incompletely mixed and contains several compartments due to faulting. ([[2001-al-subai|Al-Subai, 2001, p. 76]])
